tldr: Headlines across all 15 geographic buckets lead with the punitive frame — Yoon sentenced, convicted, jailed for 30 years — but framing diverges sharply below the surface: South Korean outlets alone surface the political motive ('drone plot to declare martial law') and the defense team's protest in headline text, while Global Times (China) uniquely headlines a separate 5-year obstruction conviction, leaving its readers without the day's primary verdict.

evidence.quote: South Korea sentences ex-president Yoon to 30 years
30 anni all'ex presidente Yoon per l'invio di droni
приговорили к 30 годам за отправку БПЛА в Пхеньян
كوريا الجنوبية: السجن 30 عاما للرئيس السابق
sentenced to 5 years in prison over obstruction of justice

frames.sub_frame: drone plot to manufacture martial law

evidence.quote: drone plot aimed at riling North Korea
Unification ministry pledges peace after Yoon convicted
Ousted South Korean President Yoon given prison term
Ordering Drone Incursions into N. Korea

single_outlet_findings.finding: The only headline in the corpus not covering the 30-year drone verdict: Global Times titled its article around a separate 5-year sentence for obstruction of justice, leaving state-adjacent Chinese readers without the day's primary conviction while all 22 other outlets headlined the drone case.

Only headline to surface the prosecution's theory in the title itself — 'drone plot aimed at riling North Korea so he could declare martial law' — making the authoritarian-power-grab framing visible at the RSS/homepage layer where all international outlets headlined the sentence quantum alone.

One of Yonhap's two headlines shifts entirely from the verdict to post-verdict governance: 'Unification ministry pledges peace after Yoon convicted in N. Korea drone case' is the only title in the corpus that foregrounds South Korea's institutional response rather than the sentencing itself.

KBS's second headline is the only title in the corpus to frame the story from the defense vantage point — 'Yoon's Legal Team Protests Heavy Sentence Handed Down in Drone Infiltration Trial' — presenting a sympathetic or adversarial-legal perspective absent from every other outlet's headline choice.

bottom_line: The headline layer unanimously advertises a criminal conviction story — 30 years for a drone operation — but South Korean domestic outlets alone embed the political motive, the defense protest, and the policy aftermath in their titles, creating a homepage framing gap between South Korean and international readers that the body text largely closes.
